name: Field Logistics & Disruption Response
description: Protocols for managing supply chain disruptions, field logistics, and resource allocation during emergencies.

documents.title: Field Logistics Disruption Response
documents.content:
FIELD LOGISTICS DISRUPTION RESPONSE PROTOCOL

1. ASSESSMENT
- Identify the nature of the disruption (transportation, supply, communication)
- Determine the scope: local, regional, or widespread
- Estimate duration of disruption
- Identify critical supply needs that must be maintained

2. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS
- Activate backup supply routes if primary routes are compromised
- Contact alternative suppliers within 100-mile radius
- Implement inventory rationing for critical supplies
- Establish communication with all field units

3. RESOURCE ALLOCATION
- Triage supplies: critical vs. non-critical
- Redistribute from low-priority to high-priority areas
- Maintain minimum 72-hour emergency reserves at all times
- Document all resource movements and expenditures

4. COORDINATION
- Liaise with neighboring agencies and mutual aid partners
- Participate in joint logistics calls every 4 hours
- Report status to incident command every 2 hours
- Maintain shared logistics dashboard with real-time inventory

5. RECOVERY
- Once primary routes are restored, replenish emergency reserves
- Conduct after-action review within 72 hours
- Update logistics plans based on lessons learned
- File reimbursement claims within 30 days
